questions.text: Who is a 'Consumer'?
options: A person who buys goods or services for use
A person who works in a factory
questions.explanation: Any individual who purchases goods or avails services for final consumption is a consumer.

questions.text: Which of the following is a common way consumers are exploited in the market?
questions.explanation: Adulteration, under-weighting, and charging more than the Maximum Retail Price (MRP) are forms of exploitation.

questions.text: The consumer movement in India as a 'social force' originated with the need to protect consumers against:
questions.explanation: The movement arose due to food shortages, hoarding, black marketing, and adulteration.

questions.text: When was the Consumer Protection Act (COPRA) enacted in India?
questions.explanation: COPRA 1986 is a significant landmark in the consumer movement in India.

questions.text: Which right ensures that a consumer is protected against goods and services that are hazardous to life and property?
questions.explanation: Right to Safety is essential for products like electrical appliances and pressure cookers.

questions.text: Why are 'ISI' or 'Agmark' logos important on products?
options: They make the product look good
They represent quality standards and safety
questions.explanation: These logos assure consumers that the organization has monitored the quality of the product.

questions.text: Under the 'Right to be Informed', consumers have the right to know about:
options: Batch number and date of manufacture
Expiry date and address of manufacturer
questions.explanation: Rules mandate that manufacturers display all essential details on the packaging.

questions.text: What does RTI stand for in the context of Indian law?
questions.explanation: The Right to Information Act (2005) ensures citizens can get information about government functions.

questions.text: The right to have access to a variety of goods and services at competitive prices is known as:
questions.explanation: Any consumer who receives a service in whatever capacity has the right to choose whether to continue to receive the service.

questions.text: If a consumer is cheated or exploited, they have the right to settle their claims against the trader. This is the:
questions.explanation: Redressal involves getting compensation for the damage or loss caused by the seller.

questions.text: How many levels of consumer courts were set up under COPRA?
questions.explanation: The three-tier quasi-judicial machinery includes District, State, and National level courts.

questions.text: Consumer disputes involving claims up to Rs. 1 crore (as per 2019 amendment) are dealt with at the:
questions.explanation: The District level court deals with cases of lower value, allowing easy access for consumers.

questions.text: The Consumer Protection Act 2019 replaced the older act of:
questions.explanation: The 2019 Act was brought in to address modern challenges like e-commerce.

questions.text: World Consumer Rights Day is celebrated every year on:
questions.explanation: March 15th is observed globally to promote consumer rights.

questions.text: National Consumer Day in India is observed on:
questions.explanation: India celebrates its own Consumer Day on December 24th, the day the 1986 Act was signed by the President.

questions.text: What is 'Jago Grahak Jago'?
options: A government awareness campaign for consumer rights
questions.explanation: It is a mass awareness campaign to inform consumers about their rights and responsibilities.

questions.text: Consumer forums or Consumer Protection Councils are ________ organizations.
questions.explanation: These councils guide consumers on how to file cases and represent them in courts.

questions.text: Which logo is used for quality certification of edible oil and food products in India?
questions.explanation: Agmark is the certification mark for agricultural products.

questions.text: Which logo is found on jewellery made of gold or silver?
questions.explanation: Hallmarking certifies the purity of precious metals.

questions.text: MRP on a product stands for:
questions.explanation: MRP is the maximum price that can be charged for a product including all taxes.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a consumer right?
questions.explanation: Consumer rights are for protection, not for exploiting others.

questions.text: The 'Right to Represent' in consumer courts allows a consumer to:
options: Appear in person and argue their own case
questions.explanation: The consumer court system is designed to be simple so that individuals can represent themselves.

questions.text: When we purchase a commodity, we find certain details on the packing. This is because of the:
questions.explanation: Manufacturers are legally required to provide information about the product.

questions.text: Rules and regulations are required for the protection of the consumers in the:
questions.explanation: Markets do not work in a fair manner when consumers are individual and weak compared to large producers.

questions.text: A consumer should always ask for a ______ after purchasing goods.
questions.explanation: A cash memo is the essential proof of purchase required to file a case in consumer court.

questions.text: Adulteration means:
questions.explanation: Adulteration lowers the quality of food and can be dangerous to health.

questions.text: Which of the following is a responsibility of a consumer?
questions.explanation: Consumers must be alert and aware to avoid being cheated.

questions.text: Consumer courts are also known as ________ courts.
questions.explanation: They follow simplified procedures and are specialized for consumer disputes.

questions.text: If a case is dismissed at the District level, where can the consumer appeal?
questions.explanation: The hierarchy of appeals follows District -> State -> National Commission.

questions.text: The main reason why the consumer movement started in India was:
questions.explanation: The movement was a result of growing anger among people against black marketing and hoarding.

questions.text: FPO mark is found on which type of products?
questions.explanation: FPO stands for Fruit Products Order, found on jams, squashes, etc.

questions.text: A manufacturer of medicines must show the ________ on the label.
questions.explanation: Medical information is vital for the safety of the consumer.

questions.text: Consumer Protection Councils are mainly involved in:
questions.explanation: They are advisory bodies that help in consumer education.

questions.text: If a consumer is denied the right to choose, it is a violation of:
questions.explanation: Right to choose is a fundamental consumer right guaranteed by law.

questions.text: Buying a pressure cooker without an ISI mark is dangerous because:
options: It may lack safety features and could burst
questions.explanation: ISI mark ensures that the product has passed safety and quality tests.

questions.text: The 'Right to Consumer Education' means consumers should:
options: Acquire knowledge and skills to be an informed consumer
questions.explanation: Informed consumers are less likely to be exploited.

questions.text: A seller cannot force a consumer to buy a specific brand if they want another. This is part of:
questions.explanation: The consumer has the final say in their purchase decision.

questions.text: Which of the following is an unfair trade practice?
questions.explanation: Misleading advertisements trick consumers into buying products that don't match the claims.

questions.text: Large companies with huge wealth and power can easily manipulate the market. This makes ________ necessary.
questions.explanation: Laws like COPRA protect the small individual consumer from the might of large corporations.

questions.text: The hallmark of a well-functioning market is that the consumer is:
questions.explanation: In a healthy economy, the interests of consumers should be prioritized.
